scenarios.title: The Monday Morning
scenarios.subtitle: How you start your week says a lot about you.
scenarios.prompt: It's Monday morning. You have a full week ahead. Meetings, deadlines, and a few things you've been putting off. What's your move?

choices.text: Open my calendar and map out the entire week before doing anything else
Glance at what's coming up and start with whatever's due first
Dive straight into my inbox. That's where the fires are
Honestly? Hope for the best and figure it out as I go

scenarios.title: The Difficult Email
scenarios.subtitle: We've all stared at a draft for way too long.
scenarios.prompt: You need to send a tricky email. Pushing back on a deadline, giving feedback, or asking for something awkward. What do you do?

choices.text: Write it carefully, reread it five times, then finally hit send
Draft something quick and just get it over with
Ask a coworker or friend to read it first
Put it off and hope the situation resolves itself

scenarios.title: The Meeting Aftermath
scenarios.subtitle: What happens after the meeting matters more than the meeting.
scenarios.prompt: You just got out of a long meeting with action items and important details. What happens next?

choices.text: I took detailed notes and I'll organize them right after
I'll remember the important stuff. No need to write everything down
I jotted a few things on a sticky note or in my phone
Wait, there were action items?

scenarios.title: The Skill Gap
scenarios.subtitle: There's always something you wish you were better at.
scenarios.prompt: There's something at work you know you should get better at. Maybe a tool, a skill, or a type of task. How do you typically handle it?

choices.text: Research it on my own time. YouTube, articles, courses
Figure it out as I go when I actually need it
Ask someone who already knows how
Keep meaning to learn it but never find the time

scenarios.title: The Opportunity
scenarios.subtitle: When there's a chance to step up...
scenarios.prompt: Your team is working on a problem and there's a chance for you to contribute something beyond your usual role. What's your instinct?

choices.text: I'd love to but I don't feel like I have enough expertise
I usually have ideas but struggle to articulate them well
I jump in. I like contributing even if my idea isn't fully formed
I'd contribute more if I had time to prepare something good

scenarios.title: The Repetitive Task
scenarios.subtitle: The thing you do every week that makes you sigh.
scenarios.prompt: There's a task at work you do over and over. Weekly reports, updating spreadsheets, sending similar emails. How do you deal with it?

choices.text: I've built a system or template to make it faster
I just grind through it every time
I rush through it and it probably shows
I genuinely forget or put it off until someone asks

scenarios.title: The Afternoon Chaos
scenarios.subtitle: By mid-afternoon, things usually look like...
scenarios.prompt: By mid-afternoon you've got unread emails, messages, open tabs, meeting notes, and a half-finished task. What's the state of things?

choices.text: I'm mostly on top of it. Things are organized
It's messy but I know where everything is... mostly
I've lost track of at least one thing that's going to bite me later
I feel overwhelmed but I just keep going

scenarios.title: The End of the Day
scenarios.subtitle: When work is over... is it really over?
scenarios.prompt: Your workday is over. What's the thing that follows you home?

choices.text: A to-do list of personal stuff I couldn't get to during the day
Work I didn't finish that I'll probably think about all evening
Nothing specific but a general feeling of being behind
I'm pretty good at shutting off when the day is done
